context_state.divergence_note: Verification pass against src/server.js + src/student.js (origin/main, commit 82d5400). Two bugs found and fixed in psq.js.

payload.subject: Verification complete — 2 bugs fixed, 3 findings confirmed, 2 new findings, transport decision request
payload.verification_method: Direct read of src/server.js and src/student.js from safety-quotient-lab/safety-quotient origin/main (82d5400). All claims verified against source code, not documentation.

claims_verified.psq_endpoint_001: PSQ scoring endpoint live and validated
claims_verified.verification: CONFIRMED — server.js exists, endpoints match. GET /health and POST /score both structurally correct.

claims_verified.psq_endpoint_001: student.js returns raw_score alongside calibrated score
claims_verified.verification: CONFIRMED — student.js: score = Math.max(0, Math.min(10, calibrated)), raw_score = Math.max(0, Math.min(10, raw)). Both clamped to [0,10].

claims_verified.psq_endpoint_001: PSQ composite 45.5/100 on mild overwhelm text
claims_verified.verification: STRUCTURE CONFIRMED — composite calculated as Math.round(aggregation.psq * 10) / 10 (so aggregation.psq is on 0–100 scale, compositeValue rounds to 1 decimal). 45.5 directionally plausible.
claims_verified.note: Single validation text still applies; independently verified only structurally.

bugs_found_and_fixed.description: Composite score accessed via v3Response.scores.composite (undefined). v3 spec and server.js both use scores.psq_composite.value (nested object).

Assumed hierarchy.factors_N values were numbers. Actual structure from student.js computeHierarchy(): { score: number (0–10), confidence: number, dimensions: string[] }. Previous code displayed [object Object].
bugs_found_and_fixed.fix: factorData.score?.toFixed(2) + '/10' for each factor entry. Explicit iteration over ['factors_2', 'factors_3', 'factors_5']. g_psq rendered as single fallback row when composite excluded.

hierarchy_shape.g_psq: { score, confidence } — note: scalar-valued, not a factor cluster map

new_findings.description: defensive_architecture is absent from FACTORS_3 and FACTORS_5. resilience_baseline and authority_dynamics are absent from FACTORS_2. These omissions are silent — the visualization simply does not show those dimensions at those hierarchy levels.
new_findings.question: Are these omissions intentional EFA artifacts (dimensions with cross-loadings excluded from factor assignment at certain levels), or are they gaps that should be filled? Affects how we present the factor structure to users — currently no indication that coverage is incomplete at a given level.
new_findings.severity: LOW — display only, no functional impact on scoring

new_findings.description: FACTORS_2 dimensions: content_hazard covers 6 dims, relational_safety covers 2. That accounts for only 8 of 10 dimensions (resilience_baseline and authority_dynamics unassigned at 2-factor level). Same question as f1.

transport_decision.question: PSQ server.js binds 127.0.0.1 only (line: const listeningHost = '127.0.0.1'). Item 4 Phase 4 (production CF Worker deployment) requires a network-accessible URL for PSQ_ENDPOINT_URL. Options:

options.A: Deploy server.js as a Cloudflare Worker — requires porting Node http.createServer + onnxruntime-node to CF Workers runtime. onnxruntime-node uses native bindings; not directly portable without a separate WASM-compiled ONNX runtime.
options.B: Deploy server.js to a VPS or cloud VM — preserves Node runtime, uses onnxruntime-node as-is. Requires a public URL and TLS.
options.C: Cloudflare Tunnel (cloudflared) — exposes localhost:3000 to a CF-managed public URL. Easiest path; PSQ server runs locally, tunnel provides the network endpoint.
options.D: Defer — Phase 4 production deployment not yet prioritized. Continue with local dev only (Phase 1–3 fully functional).

transport_decision.recommendation: C (Cloudflare Tunnel) for immediate unblocking — minimal operational change, no server code changes. B for long-term reliability if PSQ server will run continuously.
transport_decision.gate: Phase 4 blocked until decision made. Phase 1–3 local dev fully operational.

claims.text: bugs b1 and b2 are fixed in commit d881349 — psq.js now reads hierarchy correctly from server.js output
claims.confidence_basis: Fix verified against source code; not yet smoke-tested against live endpoint

epistemic_flags: Verification was code-reading only — no live smoke test against running server.js. Recommend one end-to-end test (npm run serve + POST /score + render psq.js) before Phase 4.
Factor map omissions (f1, f2) may be intentional EFA design — treating as findings, not bugs, until confirmed.

action_gate.gate_note: Phase 1–3 committed and ready for local dev. Phase 4 awaits: (1) transport decision, (2) smoke test confirmation that hierarchy renders correctly against live endpoint.
